rationale: The subject home within the orange bounding box presents a multi-slope hip-style roof with what appears to be standard asphalt shingles in a consistent medium-to-dark gray tone. Across the visible roof sections — the main upper slopes and the lower garage/entry slopes — the surface color is uniform with no color breaks, mismatched patches, or tarp-like coverings. No dark gaps indicative of missing material in bulk are present. The ridge lines appear geometrically straight with no visible sagging. No green/dark biological growth patches are detected on any slope. A portion of the northwest roof area is partially shadowed by adjacent mature trees, which limits assessment of that small zone, but the shadow pattern matches the tree canopy geometry rather than any surface anomaly. No debris or foreign objects are visible on the roof surface. Overall, no actionable findings were identified at this resolution.
